segments.text: First listen and then answer the question.

translations.zh-Hans: 先听录音，然后回答问题。

segments.text: Who will be driving the buses next week?

translations.zh-Hans: 下周谁来驾驶公共汽车？

segments.text: Busmen have decided to go on strike next week.

translations.zh-Hans: 公共汽车司机决定下周举行罢工。

segments.text: The strike is due to begin on Tuesday.

translations.zh-Hans: 罢工计划于星期二开始。

segments.text: No one knows how long it will last.
The busmen have stated that the strike will continue until general agreement is reached about pay and working conditions.

translations.zh-Hans: 公交司机们表示，在工资和工作条件问题达成全面协议之前，罢工将继续下去。

segments.text: Most people believe that the strike will last for at least a week.

translations.zh-Hans: 大多数人认为罢工至少会持续一周。

segments.text: Many owners of private cars are going to offer free rides to people on their way to work.

translations.zh-Hans: 许多私家车主准备让上班途中的人免费搭车。

segments.text: This will relieve pressure on the trains to some extent.

translations.zh-Hans: 这将在一定程度上缓解火车的压力。

segments.text: Meanwhile, a number of university students have volunteered to drive buses while the strike lasts.

translations.zh-Hans: 一些大学生已自愿在罢工期间驾驶公共汽车。

segments.text: All the students are expert drivers, but before they drive any of the buses, they will have to pass a special test.

translations.zh-Hans: 这些学生都是熟练的司机，但驾驶任何公共汽车之前，都必须通过一项特别考试。

segments.text: The students are going to take the test in two days' time.

translations.zh-Hans: 学生们将在两天后参加考试。

segments.text: Even so, people are going to find it difficult to get to work.

translations.zh-Hans: 即便如此，人们仍会觉得上班很困难。

segments.text: But so far, the public has expressed its gratitude to the students in letters to the press.

translations.zh-Hans: 不过到目前为止，公众已经通过致信报社表达了对学生们的感谢。

segments.text: Only one or two people have objected that the students will drive too fast.

translations.zh-Hans: 只有一两个人提出异议，担心学生们会把车开得太快。
